free_text: Neutron production from spallation of proton
on a tungsten target. The 800 MeV proton beam produced
by the LANSCE accelerator can be directed to the LSDS
of two parallel plate ionization chambers with the
fissionable sample in one of the chambers.
consists of three stainless steel plates, each 3.15 cm
in diameter, mounted on ceramic rods and spaced 2 cm
The compensated fission chamber was tested in the LSDS
with 9.87 ng and 27 ng of 239Pu, 0.8 cm in diameter,
deposited onto a titanium foil of 3.4 cm in diameter,
Data received from D. Rochman and C. Romano on
